programmes.show_description: (2005) Violent action-thriller starring Jason Statham as the tough courier Frank Martin, who's hired to guard a government drugs czar's son. But his mission soon lurches towards disaster.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(1955) Romantic drama with Jane Wyman and Rock Hudson. A wealthy widow decides to restart her life. But her children and her social set disapprove of her lover, forcing her to rethink.
(1957) Second World War drama. Deborah Kerr plays a nun stranded on a Japanese-held Pacific island, with an untalkative marine (Robert Mitchum) as her only companion and hope of salvation.

programmes.show_name: The True Story of Jesse James
programmes.show_description: (1957) Robert Wagner stars as the infamous outlaw in Nicholas Ray's western, which follows the last 18 years of the robber's life.
(1947) Supernatural romance starring Rex Harrison as the spirit of a gruff sea captain who haunts widow Gene Tierney, only for her to fall in love with him. Directed by Joseph L Mankiewicz.
(2019) Will Smith stars in Ang Lee's action-thriller as both an over-the-hill hitman and the young clone of himself sent to take him out. With Clive Owen.
(2023) Molly Gordon and Nick Lieberman's fabulously quirky mockumentary about a kids theater (sic) where it's difficult to tell who the adults are and where bad acting is a given.
(2002) Action thriller starring Vin Diesel as Xander Cage: a thrill-seeking extreme sports enthusiast, stuntman and rebellious athlete-turned-reluctant spy. With Samuel L Jackson.
(2022) Adults only drama tackling youth violence in Iceland. When charismatic, latently sensitive Addi welcomes bullied Balli into his gang, the question is why? In Icelandic/subs.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(1954) Western with Robert Mitchum and Marilyn Monroe. An ex-con and a saloon singer steer a raft through hostile territory to face a showdown with her crooked fiancé. With Rory Calhoun.
(1957) Second World War thriller. Robert Mitchum stars as the captain of a US destroyer in the North Atlantic hunting down a German U-boat and its world-weary captain, Curt Jurgens.
(1959) Comedy western with Kenneth More. An English gunsmith decides the Wild West is the best market for his wares, but little does he expect to end up sheriff of a lawless frontier town.
Alexander Skarsgard, Anya Taylor-Joy and director Robert Eggers talk about making their Viking adventure.
(1950) Classic comedy with James Stewart and Josephine Hull. A middle-aged eccentric insists his best friend is a six-foot invisible rabbit. And that's driving his sister to distraction.
(2020) Comedy-drama with Liam Neeson. An estranged father and son team up to fix up and sell an old Tuscan villa that their late wife and mother left to them.
(1993) Brian De Palma's crime thriller with Al Pacino and Sean Penn. An ex-con, fresh out of jail, flamboyantly declares his plan to go straight. But his criminal past won't let him go...
(2019) Wickedly funny comedy horror starring Samara Weaving. A bride finds herself being hunted down by her new in-laws when she's asked to observe a deadly family tradition.
